{{Quote|The one that almost got you dropped that big black hole on his tail as Eleven was closing. Dinger flew into it and never knew what hit him.|Major Alinn Varth|<ref name="dt1" />}}
'''Dinger''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who was serving in [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], a famed [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] unit, as of [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. Assigned the [[Callsign/Legends|callsign]] [[Rogue Eleven/Legends|Rogue Eleven]], Dinger flew under the command of [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Gavin Darklighter]]. In the early months of 25 ABY, Rogue Squadron was assigned to the [[New Republic First Fleet]], led by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Traest Kre'fey]], and was tasked with investigating reports of an [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. During Rogue Squadron's first encounter with the threat, Dinger was [[Death/Legends|killed]], falling prey to the biotechnology of the alien [[Yuuzhan Vong]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Heads up, Rogues, we have contacts at 354 mark 20. S-foils in attack position.|Gavin Darklighter issues orders during Dinger's last engagement|<ref name="dt1" />}}
Dinger trained as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Corps]] and by [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] had been inducted into [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], a starfighter unit famed for its role in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Assigned the [[Callsign/Legends|callsign]] [[Rogue Eleven/Legends|Rogue Eleven]], Dinger flew alongside [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Alinn Varth]] in the squadron's third [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] group, although the unit itself was under the overall command of veteran pilot [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Gavin Darklighter]].<ref name="dt1" />

In 25 ABY, Dinger and his fellow Rogue Squadron pilots were assigned to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic First Fleet|First Fleet]]. The [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]], [[Traest Kre'fey]], wished to investigate rumors of an [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] that had purportedly occurred in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and requested Darklighter's aid in responding to the threat. Rogue Squadron was stationed on Kre'fey's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Bothan Assault Cruiser]] ''[[Ralroost]]''. Upon reports of [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] activity in an Outer Rim [[Star system/Legends|star system]], Darklighter led the squadron out into [[Realspace/Legends|space]] to reconnoiter the situation.<ref name="dt1" />

Major [[Inyri Forge]] soon detected unknown signatures in the system's [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] field, which were soon confirmed to be [[Ugly/Legends|Uglies]], starfighters cobbled together out of various parts. As Dinger and his fellow pilots engaged the Uglies, it soon became clear that the starfighters had already been heavily damaged in an engagement against an unknown foe. Darklighter made contact with a [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B escort frigate]] which served as the pirates' base of operations and successfully convinced [[Urias Xhaxin|the captain]] of the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] to order the pirates to stand down. The pirate [[Captain/Legends|captain]] later revealed that his forces had been attacked by unfamiliar starships, which Admiral Kre'fey concluded to have been the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], members of the alien [[Species/Legends|species]] that he was investigating.<ref name="dt1" />

As Kre'fey set up a reconnaissance mission of the star system where the pirates claimed to have been ambushed, Dinger and his fellow pilots ran [[Skipchaser|simulations]] of engagements with Yuuzhan Vong starfighters—termed [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]]—in order to practice their skills against the hitherto unencountered opponent. With the admiral's first reconnaissance mission inconclusive, Colonel Darklighter led Dinger and the other pilots out to the star system alongside a [[T-65BR X-wing reconnaissance starfighter]] in order to take another set of readings. Upon emerging from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the system, Dinger fell into position alongside Major Varth and provided cover for the reconnaissance starfighter.<ref name="dt1" />

Soon, six coralskippers, as the organic Yuuzhan Vong starfighters had been termed, emerged from the system's asteroid field and engaged Rogue Squadron. Dinger and his fellow pilots were prepared to combat the organic and unfamiliar starfighters to some extent, due to the simulations they had flown. Nevertheless, as the Yuuzhan Vong coralskippers began deploying miniature [[Black hole/Legends|black holes]] to strip the New Republic starfighters' [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and counteract [[Laser/Legends|laser]] blasts and [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] strikes, the Rogue Squadron pilots had to field new tactics to overcome the biotechnology. While closing in upon an elite coralskipper pilot who was pursuing Colonel Darklighter, both Dinger and [[Rogue Twelve]], [[Tik (Rogue Squadron)|Tik]], failed to evade the coralskipper's black hole defense in time. The gravitic singularity tore Dinger's starfighter apart, instantly killing the Rogue Squadron pilot. Tik was killed after going extravehicular.<ref name="dt1" />

==Personality and traits==
Dinger flew in Rogue Squadron, a New Republic starfighter squadron with a reputation for piloting excellence. Gavin Darklighter, as Rogue Leader, was proud of all the pilots in his squadron and felt that they were skilful and driven.<ref name="dt1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dinger was a minor Rogue Squadron character in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'', authored by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and published in [[2000]]. The pilot was later given a sentence-long entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which was published in [[2008]].
